perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees: (a) to hold Premisys' Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties (other than on a confidential basis to its Representatives in furtherance of the <PAGE> Business Purpose); and (c) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees: (a) to hold Premisys' Sensitive Data in strict confidence; (b) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties (other than on a confidential basis to its Representatives in furtherance of the <PAGE> Business Purpose); and (c) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing (a) to refer to "Sensitive Data" while (b) and (c) continue to use "Confidential Information" introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 2. This creates ambiguity about which data is subject to which restrictions, and makes it unclear if the term 'Confidential Information' and 'Sensitive Data' is reffering to the same information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees: (a) to hold Premisys' Sensitive Data in strict confidence; (b) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties (other than on a confidential basis to its Representatives in furtherance of the <PAGE> Business Purpose); and (c) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose. Recipient may disclose Premisys' Confidential Information to its employees with a bona fide need to know, but only to the extent reasonably necessary to carry out the Business Purpose. Recipient agrees to instruct all such employees that they may not use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Business Purpose and (except as permitted by the terms of this Agreement) not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties, including consultants, without the prior written consent of Premisys.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the initial term from 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data' in clause (a), while clauses (b) and (c) still refer to 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text also uses both terms interchangeably. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because the contract now refers to two potentially different data types, 'Confidential Information' and 'Sensitive Data', but it is not clarified if those terms are referring to the same data or not. This leads to ambiguity and uncertainty about which data is subject to which restrictions, fitting the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will not, without the prior written consent of Premisys, disclose to any third party (except as permitted by the terms of this Agreement) the fact that any Confidential Information has been disclosed hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that Recipient (and its Representatives) may make such disclosure if, in the reasonable opinion of counsel for such party, such disclosure is required by law, regulation or any stock exchange or the Nasdaq National Market.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will not, without the prior written consent of Premisys, disclose to any third party (except as permitted by the terms of this Agreement) the fact that any Private Data has been disclosed hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that Recipient (and its Representatives) may make such disclosure if, in the reasonable opinion of counsel for such party, such disclosure is required by law, regulation or any stock exchange or the Nasdaq National Market.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Private Data" in Section 3 while other sections still refer to "Confidential Information" creates an in-text contradiction. It becomes unclear whether the restriction on disclosing the fact that information was disclosed applies only to "Private Data" or also to "Confidential Information".
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN/DESTRUCTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Premisys may, at any time, deliver written notice (a "TERMINATION NOTICE") to Recipient of Premisys' election to have all tangible materials (including without limitation paper and magnetic storage media) in the possession of Recipient (or its Representatives) which contain, reflect, are based upon or derived from, in whole or in part, any of Premisys' Confidential Information ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS") either returned to Premisys or, at Premisys' election, destroyed. Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient shall, within twenty (20) calendar days, either: (i) return the Confidential Materials in its possession to Premisys and instruct its Representatives to do the same; (ii) destroy the Confidential Materials in a secure manner and instruct its Representatives to do the same; or (iii) at Premisys' election, take any combination of steps (i) and (ii). Prior to the expiration of the foregoing twenty (20) period, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5 (a "COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE"). Notwithstanding the -2- <PAGE> provisions of this Section 5, if Recipient receives a Termination Notice, it shall not be required to return or destroy Confidential Materials to the extent that, in the reasonable opinion of legal counsel for such party, such return or destruction violates any law or regulation; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that in such event Recipient shall describe in its Compliance Certificate the extent to which it is not returning or destroying Confidential Materials. Notwithstanding any return or destruction of Confidential Materials, Recipient will continue to be bound by its obligations under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information contained or reflected in such Confidential Materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', and the contradicted text also refers to 'Confidential Information'. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Data' in Section 3. The contradicted text in Section 5, which concerns the return/destruction of materials, specifically mentions 'Confidential Information' and obligations related to it. This creates a contradiction because it's no longer clear whether the obligations in Section 5 apply to 'Private Data' as well, creating uncertainty about which type of information is subject to the return/destruction requirements. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', which highlights the use of multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that Recipient or any of its Representatives is requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Confidential Information of Premisys, Recipient shall provide Premisys with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement so that Premisys may seek a protective order, confidential treatment or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that Recipient or any of its Representatives is requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Protected Information of Premisys, Recipient shall provide Premisys with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement so that Premisys may seek a protective order, confidential treatment or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Protected Information" in Section 4, while other sections retain the term "Confidential Information," creates an in-text contradiction. It raises uncertainty about which type of information triggers the notification requirement. The requirements will be then contradicted in the rest of the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees: (a) to hold Premisys' Sensitive Data in strict confidence; (b) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties (other than on a confidential basis to its Representatives in furtherance of the <PAGE> Business Purpose); and (c) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose. Recipient may disclose Premisys' Confidential Information to its employees with a bona fide need to know, but only to the extent reasonably necessary to carry out the Business Purpose. Recipient agrees to instruct all such employees that they may not use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Business Purpose and (except as permitted by the terms of this Agreement) not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties, including consultants, without the prior written consent of Premisys.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Protected Information" creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which repeatedly refers to "Confidential Information". This misalignment makes it unclear whether the obligations in the contradicted text apply to 'Protected Information' as well, creating ambiguity and a direct conflict regarding which information the recipient must keep confidential and not disclose. This aligns with the provided definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Premisys may, at any time, deliver written notice (a "TERMINATION NOTICE") to Recipient of Premisys' election to have all tangible materials (including without limitation paper and magnetic storage media) in the possession of Recipient (or its Representatives) which contain, reflect, are based upon or derived from, in whole or in part, any of Premisys' Confidential Information ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS") either returned to Premisys or, at Premisys' election, destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Premisys may, at any time, deliver written notice (a "TERMINATION NOTICE") to Recipient of Premisys' election to have all tangible materials (including without limitation paper and magnetic storage media) in the possession of Recipient (or its Representatives) which contain, reflect, are based upon or derived from, in whole or in part, any of Premisys' Proprietary Information ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS") either returned to Premisys or, at Premisys' election, destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Proprietary Information" in Section 5, makes the term "Confidential Materials" unclear and contradicting. Is it confidential materials based upon confidential information or proprietary information. It generates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN/DESTRUCTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Premisys may, at any time, deliver written notice (a "TERMINATION NOTICE") to Recipient of Premisys' election to have all tangible materials (including without limitation paper and magnetic storage media) in the possession of Recipient (or its Representatives) which contain, reflect, are based upon or derived from, in whole or in part, any of Premisys' Proprietary Information ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS") either returned to Premisys or, at Premisys' election, destroyed. Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient shall, within twenty (20) calendar days, either: (i) return the Confidential Materials in its possession to Premisys and instruct its Representatives to do the same; (ii) destroy the Confidential Materials in a secure manner and instruct its Representatives to do the same; or (iii) at Premisys' election, take any combination of steps (i) and (ii). Prior to the expiration of the foregoing twenty (20) period, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5 (a "COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE"). Notwithstanding the -2- <PAGE> provisions of this Section 5, if Recipient receives a Termination Notice, it shall not be required to return or destroy Confidential Materials to the extent that, in the reasonable opinion of legal counsel for such party, such return or destruction violates any law or regulation; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that in such event Recipient shall describe in its Compliance Certificate the extent to which it is not returning or destroying Confidential Materials. Notwithstanding any return or destruction of Confidential Materials, Recipient will continue to be bound by its obligations under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information contained or reflected in such Confidential Materials.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies "Confidential Information" to "Proprietary Information" in the definition of "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS". This change creates an in-text contradiction because the contradicted text refers to the return/destruction of "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS" which is now defined based on 'Proprietary Information', leading to confusion about whether the obligation applies to materials based on Confidential or Proprietary Information or both, therefore creating a contradiction within the contract itself, as stated in the explanation. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
